examples:
Command Example
!abuseipdb-get-categories
Human Readable Output
6. Get the full list of false positive IP addresses
Returns the False Positive List (FPL) from abuse.ch, containing indicators (IPs and domains) that have been removed from their blocklists.
Base Command
abuseipdb-get-fplist
Input

!abuseipdb-get-categories
Human Readable Output
6. Get the full list of false positive IP addresses
Returns the False Positive List (FPL) from abuse.ch, containing indicators (IPs and domains) that have been removed from their blocklists.
Base Command
abuseipdb-get-fplist
Input
Argument Name

Command Example
!abuseipdb-get-fplist format="json" limit=10
Additional Information
What is the "Confidence of Abuse" rating, and how is it calculated?
AbuseIPDB confidence of abuse is a rating (0-100) of how confident we are, based on user reports, that an IP address is completely malicious. A rating of 100 means we are certain that an IP address is malicious, and a rating of 0 means we have no reason to suspect it is malicious.

name: abuseipdb-get-fplist

outputs.description: The unique identifier for the False Positive List entry. AbuseIPDB.FPL.time_stamp Date
outputs.name: AbuseIPDB.FPL.id

outputs.description: AbuseIPDB.FPL.platform The platform or service associated with the entry. AbuseIPDB.FPL.entry_type
outputs.name: The date and time (UTC) when the entry was added to the False Positive List.

outputs.description: The type of the indicator (e.g., IPv4, domain). AbuseIPDB.FPL.entry_value The actual indicator value (IP address or domain) that was marked as a false positive.
outputs.name: String

outputs.description: The entity or user who requested the removal of the indicator from the blocklist. AbuseIPDB.FPL.removal_notes Additional context or justification for why the indicator was removed.
outputs.name: AbuseIPDB.FPL.removed_by
